def: adj. & adv. = beg, q.v.; — s.m. Courier, express; a hawk.
adj. = begī, q.v.
s.f. A young lady of rank.
s.f. The wife of a beg; lady.
adj. Good; better ( = běh-tar)
subs: běh-būdī, s.f. Well-being, welfare; health, vigour (syn. běh-tarī); běh-būdī-ě-ḵẖalāʼiq, s.f. Public benefit or good.

def: s.m. Perforation, hole.
s.m. = bihān, q.v.
s.f. Nursery for rice plants; advances given for seed-gram.
adj. & s.f. = behaṛ, q.v.

adj. & s.f. Uncultivated; uneven (land), rugged, rough, cut up, raviny; — broken ground, land broken into ravines, rugged or rough land or soil; sterile land, land not brought under cultivation, waste land; jungle, forest.
s.f. See s.v. بہہ běh.
s.f. Earthen vessel presented by the potters of a village to a newly-married pair.
s.m. corr. of P. bīlā, belā, q.v.
s.m. Carter (of cotton; = biknā or běhnā, q.v.).
s.m. Nur sery for rice plants ( = behan).
s.m. Grass kept for pasturage.
s.f. Subscription, &c. = behrī, q.v.
s.f. Contribution, subscription, instalment; assessment on a share; distribution of an aggregate sum; cess, impost ( = bihrī), behrī bāṅdhnā, v.n. To raise money by subscription; to subscribe
subs: s.m. Collection on shares of the revenue in kind
s.f. An allowance for repairing roads, &c.
s.m. The holder of a share called behrī.

def: adj. Better, superior, more worthy; good, excellent; well; preferable: advisable
subs: adj. Far better, &c.; — běhtar jānnā, v.t. To consider better; to prefer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِهْتَر (bihtar). False cognate of English better.

def: s.f. Welfare, good, advantage; improvement; superior excellence, goodness, merit, virtue.
adj. Best, superior, most excellent.
s.m. (Poet. or emphat.) = beh, q.v.
adj. Absurd, vain, idle, unprofitable, fruitless; senseless, foolish, silly, stupid, nonsensical, frivolous; indecent, improper, obscene, foul, gross, immoral
subs: s.m. Talker of nonsense, babbler; coarse or obscene talker, foul-mouthed fellow
s.f. Idle talk, talking nonsense, absurdity, frivolity; obscene talk, scurrility.

def: adj. contract. of be-hūda, q.v.
s.f. Absurdity, foolishness, folly, foolery; frivolity nonsense, vanity, unprofitableness.
Poet. contrac. of be-hosh, q.v.s.v. be.
s.m. Butt or mark for archers.
s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).
s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).
adj. Mixed; of a mixed race, mongrel.
s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).
s.m. = bijjū, q.v.
s.m. = bīj-wār, q.v.s.v. bīj.
s.m. (?), A kind of grass (growing in low ground: it is used for fodder).
adj. See s.v. be.
s.f. Root (syn. jaṛ); source, origin, extraction; foundation
subs: s.f. Extraction, lineage, descent; foundation, basis
s.m. Extirpator, exterminator; a judge (as extirpating thieves); a thief (as destroying the subjects)
s.f. Tearing up by the root, eradication, extirpation, extermination
(-kī), To root up, destroy, eradicate, extirpate, exterminate

def: s.m. = bhes, q.v.
s.m. = bhes, q.v.
s.m. The Arabian jasmine, Jasminum zambac; — vessel, well; spot in which the receiving pans are placed when sugar is manufactured
subs: s.m. Jasmine shrub or creeper (see s.v. bel, 'creeper').

def: s.m. Spade, shovel, hoe, mattock, pickaxe; line of a road or a walk marked out with a spade; border; pole for directing a boat (cf. ballī)
subs: s.m. Digger, one who uses a spade, pioneer.

def: s.f. Any climbing or creeping plant, creeper, climber, vine; tendril; offspring, issue, descendants; strings, rows, or festoons of leaves, &c. hung round a room or tent; embroidery, flowered tissue; cutaneous disease, eruption; — money given by the spectators to a dancing-girl, or to a player
subs: s.m. Shrubs and creepers; flowers or embroidery (on cloth, &c.): flowered border
v.n. To bear fruit, have offspring; to attain one's precise or utmost wish
v.n. A creeper to climb to the top (of); to reach the top or summit (of); to come to perfection; to flourish, prosper.

def: s.m. The wood-apple or Bel-tree, Ӕgle marmelos, and its fruit
subs: bel-pattar, s.m., bel-pattī, s.f. Leaf of the Bel-tree (employed as an offering to Śiva)
s.m. Fruit of the Bel-tree, wood-apple
s.f. Pulp of the Bel or wood-apple (used medicinally for diarrhœa and dysentery).

def: s.f. Time, while, period, moment, season.
s.m. The shrub or flower Jasminum zambac; shallow cup (syn. kaṭorā); an instrument of music resembling a fiddle.
s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
s.m. A cylinder of wood, metal, or stone for levelling walks, spreading dough, or inking the surface of type; roller; rolling-pin.
v.t. (Prov.), To collect cattle.
s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
s.f. Creeper, &c. ( = bel, q.v.).
s.m. Preserver, guardian, protector, supporter (applied to the Deity).
s.m. Cattle-breedor; grazier.
v.t. To spread out, roll into a flat cake, to laminate.
s.m. A cylinder of wood, metal, or stone for levelling walks, spreading dough, or inking the surface of type; roller; rolling-pin.
s.f. A small rolling-pin; a bolt; a branch.
adj. & s.m. Rolling; a roller.
s.f. Stepmother
subs: s.m. Brother born of a different mother by the same father, half-brother (by the father's side).

def: s.m. Ant's nest.
s.f. Reed, bamboo; reed-pipe, pipe, flute, fife.
s.m. = bihnā; behnā, q.v.
s.m. A fan ( = bījnā).
s.m. The grass Andropogov muricatum (commonly called ḵẖas).
v.t. = bećnā, q.v.

ety: fr. S. वे; cf. bīṅḍī, benī
def: v.t. To plait, braid; to fold (beṅḍhnā).
v.t. = beṅdhnā, q.v.
s.m. Frog, paddock, toad (syn. meṅḍak).
s.m. Grain advanced to cultivators, seed lent for sowing.

s.f. Braided hair, the hair twisted into a single unornamented braid and allowed to fall on the back (as worn by widows and women whose husbands are absent, as a token of mourning; — syn. bīṅḍā); the conflux or meeting of two or more streams in a common point of union (as that of the Ganges and Jamna at Allahabad)
subs: s.m. A particular square-shaped idol or stone image of a god at Allahabad; a proper name.

def: s.f. Dim. of benā, q.v.
s.m. = binjan, q.v.
s.f. Cane, &c. = bet, q.v.
s.m. The circular or turned handle of any weapon or instrument; handle.
s.f. Time, turn, bout, vicissitude; space of time, interval; delay, while (syn. bār)
subs: adv. Time after time, again and again, repeatedly, frequently, often.

def: s.m. Enclosure, fence, &c. ( = beṛā, beṛhā, q.v.)
s.m. The jujube tree, Zizyphus jujuba, or Z. scandens; the fruit of the jujube, plum; the lote-fruit, Rhamnus zizyphus (A. syn. kunār)
subs: s.m. lit. 'Plum and seed'; the whole, everything, all together, good and bad; hodge-podge
To make a mess (of), to spoil, complicate, confuse.

def: s.m. Enclosure, yard, court; fence, hedge, paling, railing ( = beṛhā); siege, blockade, body of soldiers, troop, army; band, association, company, party
subs: (-kā), To form an army, collect a body or crowd, &c.

def: s.m. Raft, float; boat; fleet (of boats or ships); timber formed into a raft to be brought down a river; the raft floated by the Mohammadans in honour of Ḵẖẉāja Ḵẖiẓr. (Besides the anniversary beṛā offered to propitiate him, hundreds of smaller ones with lamps may be seen on Thursdays of the month Bhādoṅ particularly, which are offered by the Mohammadans to this saint)
subs: beṛā pār lagānā, v.t. To ferry over a raft or boat; to help one over (a difficulty), remove the difficulties (of, -kā)
v.n. To tide over a difficulty, misfortune, or distress; to get well through a business, to be successful.

def: adj. = birānā, q.v.
s.m. Grove of ber trees (see ber).
s.m. Surrounding, &c. (see beṛhnā); cattle surrounded and carried off (by an enemy) by force
subs: v.t. To carry off cattle by force.

def: s.m. Enclosure; fence, &c. = beṛā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Crooked; zigzag; — beṛhe, adv. Crookedly, zigzag (cf. beṅḍā).
s.f. = beṛwīṅ, q.v.
s.f. = beṛwīṅ, q.v.
v.t. To enclose with a fence; to envelop; to surround, besiege; to drive or carry off cattle (as in a foray); to pound (cattle, &c.).
s.f. Name of a class of singers in Mirzapur.
s.f. The jujube tree, Zizyphus jujuba ( = ber).

s.f. Ring worn round the ankle; irons fastened to the legs of criminals, or of a quadruped; fetters; thread tied on the ankles, a dark blue thread or cord tied round the ankles of children (as a charm); (met.) nuptial tie, wedlock; — basket used to irrigate fields with
subs: (-meṅ), beṛiyāṅ pahnānā, beṛiyāṅ ḍālnā, To put fetters (on the feet of), to put in chains; to cause to be put in chains; — beṛiyāṅ paṛnā, v.n. To be put in irons; to be chained; to be tied in wedlock
(-kī), To have the fetters or chains taken off; to be freed from restraint, to be discharged or released.

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Hindūs.
s.f. dim. of beṛā, q.v.
s.m. Sum total, entire revenue of a farm; a kind of government tax.
s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
prep. & adv. Without, on the outside, out ( = bāhar)
subs: vulg. berūn-jāt, s.m. Suburbs; country (in opp. to town)
adj. Of or pertaining to the suburbs or country, rustic, rural; country-bred; — s.m. Rustic, country-man.

def: adj. Outer, external, &c. = bāharī, q.v.
s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
= besh; and bhes; qq.v.
s.m. = bhes, q.v.
s.m. Flour or meal of pulse (particularly of ćanā; commonly used as a substitute for soap).
adj. & s.f. Made of, or mixed with, besan; — bread, &c. made of besan and wheat-flour.
s.m. A mule.
s.m. Small heavy nose-ring (syn. nak-besar).
adj. More; greater; good, proper, well; better, superior, excellent, preferable; elegant; delightful
subs: adj. & s.m. Very much, a great deal
besh qīmat, adj. Of great price or value, costly, precious
adj. & adv. Not much, moderate quantity; more or less.

def: s.m. See بيشه besha.

ety: Pehl. veshak; Zend varesha; S. vr̤iksha
def: s.m. Forest, jungle; desert, wilderness.
s.f. Excess, surplus, increase; premium; increased assessment
subs: s.f. Increase of revenue on that of the year preceding
s.f. Increase in the lands under cultivation

def: adj. & adv. More, most; better; exceeding; — mostly, for the most part, usually, generally; rather (syn. aks̤ar).
s.m. Name of a celebrated mountain in Persia which Farhād dug through at the command of his mistress Shīrīn.
s.f. Cake made of besan or the meal of pulse.
s.m.f. (?), A small species of falcon; merlin; sparrow-hawk (syn. śikrā. Forbes, on what authority I cannot discover, states that it is the female of the species called dhotī).
s.m.f. (?), A small species of falcon; merlin; sparrow-hawk (syn. śikrā. Forbes, on what authority I cannot discover, states that it is the female of the species called dhotī).
s.f. Prostitute, harlot, courtesan; crafty or cunning woman.
s.f. = beswā, q.v.
s.f. Cane, ratan, Calamus rotang; blow or stroke with a cane
s.m. Son, boy, child; a form of address used by faqīrs towards their ćelās or disciples
subs: betā kar-lenā, v.t. To adopt a son (syn. god lenā)
s.m. A father who survives his son
s.m. (f. -ī), Grandfather; a man who has children

def: s.m. Sprite, goblin (especially one that is supposed to haunt cemeteries and animate corpses); a dead body occupied and animated by a goblin
subs: s.f. Name of a work in Hindī (so called because it contains twenty-five tales related by a baitāl).

def: s.m. Hire, wages, pittance, reward; profession, livelihood, subsistence; silver.
s.m. = beṭnan, q.v.
s.f. (?), Sandy unproductive soil.
s.m. The envelope in which cloth, lace, &c. purchased is folded up, and to which the purchaser is entitled; pack-cloth, wrapper.
s.f. Daughter, girl
subs: (-ko), To give one's daughter (to, in marriage)

def: s.f. = biṭiyā; beṭī, qq.v.
s.m. Cane; willow
subs: s.f. Allowance for mats or wicker works for packing goods.

def: s.m. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.f. Widow ( = P. بيوه bewa, q.v.).
s.f. Widow ( = bewā).
s.f. Widowhood ( = bewā-pā, bewā-pan)
s.m. The vehicle or self-moving car of a Hindū deity; &c. ( = bimān q.v.).
s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. (local) = byorā, q.v.
s.f. Widow ( = bewa)
part. (of beḵẖtan; rt. Zend vij; S. vij or vić), Sifting; diffusing, dispersing (used in comp.).
adj. See s.v. be.
s.m. The letter or sound bh.
s.f. Sudden bursting forth of flame, or steam, &c.; forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe; sudden emission of stench or offensive smell.

v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
s.m. A grass of which rope is made; a species of nettle; — light black soil; — name of a large forest at the foot of the Sewalik hills.
s.m. = bhabbhar, q.v.
adj. Fat, corpulent.
s.f. = bhabak, q.v.
v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v.
s.m. Disquietude, solicitude ( = khaṭkā), dread, alarm; panic, stampede; tumult, uproar
subs: (-meṅ), To be alarmed; to be panic-stricken; to take to flight.

def: v.n. To be solicitous; to be alarmed or frightened, to take fright (cf. bhabhar paṛnā).

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
v.n. To swell (particularly the face = bharbharānā, q.v.); to feel doubt or solicitude, &c. ( = bhabharnā, q.v.).
adj. Flaming, blazing, ablaze; red (as a live coal); white, shining, bright, glowing, refulgent, splendid, beautiful; — s.m. Blaze, flame; explosion
subs: v.n. To turn red (or livid) with rage.

def: s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
adj. = bhāvī, q.v.

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
s.m. Blast from a furnace; alembic, still; — a kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.
v.t. To heat, kindle, light; to cause to boil, &c.; to cause to blaze up suddenly; to provoke, enrage, exasperate; to excite, stimulate; to spur a horse; &c. (see bhabaknā).
s.f. Threat, menace.
s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v.
adj. = bhabhūkā, q.v.
s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v.
s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v.
adj. Alarmed, startled, staggered, amazed, stunned, aghast, scared
subs: v.n. To be astonished, amazed, staggered, &c.

def: v.n. To be astonished or amazed, &c. = bhaćak rahnā, q.v.s.v. bhaćak.
s.m. Sphere of the stars; circle of constellations, the zodiac.
s.m. = bhakshaṇ, q.v.
s.m. = bhakshak, q.v.
s.m. = bhakshak, q.v.
s.m. = bhakshī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = bhaksh, q.v.
v.t. = bhakshnā, q.v.
v.t. To strike with amazement, to amaze, astound, stagger, startle, scare.
v.t. = bhakshnā, q.v.
s.m. Soft-sounding blow, thud, thump; crash; sound of a falling body on soft ground (more com. bhadākā)
subs: s.m. Reiterated thuds, &c. (see bhad); closely consecutive sounds of soft bodies (such as fruit, &c.) in falling
adv. With a thud; with a crash.

def: s.m. A kind of grass which grows in poor soils (used as fodder).
s.m. Cutting of grain when it is only half ripe.
adj. & s.f. Relating to the month of Bhādoṅ, or to the harvest gathered in that month; — the autumnal crop ( = bhādoṅwī).
s.m. = bhad, q.v.
s.m. = bhad, q.v.
s.m. A term of respect applied to a Buddhist; a buddhist mendicant.
s.m. = bhaṛariyā; bhanḍeriyā, qq.v.
s.m. = bhādoṅ, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. Sound made by the repeated falling of fruit from a tree.

v.t. To produce a sound or thud (or a succession of sounds), by striking two bodies together; to strike repeatedly; to shake fruit from a tree; to cut half-ripe grain; — s.m. Shaking fruit from a tree; cutting half-ripe grain.
adj. Dull, heavy, stupid, senseless; clumsy, awkward, ungainly, ill-made, ugly; fat, heavy, unwieldy; slow, sluggish.
s.m. = bhaṛariyā; bhanḍeriyā, qq.v.
adj. = next, q.v.
adj. = next, q.v.
Ill-shaped, ill-formed, awkward, &c. = bhaddā, q.v.
Ill-shaped, ill-formed, awkward, &c. = bhaddā, q.v.
s.f. (local), Pilfering, theft.
s.m. Attendant, servant; hero.
s.m. The fifth month of the Hindūs (corresponding to a period in our calendar from about the middle of August to the middle of September), when the moon is full near the wing of Pegasus
subs: s.f. Heavy August rains, which fill the tanks, &c., and flood the fields
bhādoṅ-ke saṛakke, s.m. Light showers in the month of Bhādoṅ.

def: adj. & s.m. Relating or pertaining to the month Bhādoṅ, or to the harvest gathered in that month; — the autumnal crop ( = bhādaʼī).
s.m. The month Bhādoṅ, q.v.
subs: s.f. A name common to the third and fourth lunar asterisms or Nakshatras ( = bhadra-padā).

def: s.f. Unlucky moment; bad luck; the second, seventh, and twelfth days of a lunar fortnight; the celestial Ganges; (fem. of bhadra) an excellent female, &c.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Making beautiful, or pure'; shaving.

def: Name of a bird, a species of lark.

adj. & s.m. Good, well, prosperous, happy, auspicious, fortunate, lucky; favourable, propitious, gracious, kind, friendly, benevolent, pious, virtuous, pure, excellent, worthy; beloved, dear; an excellent person; fortune-teller; impostor, hypocrite; — prosperity, happiness, welfare, good fortune, auspiciousness; — a fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus; gold; iron or steel; name of the seventh of the eleven astronomical periods called karanas; — a bullock; a water-wagtail; a heap; an epithet of Śiva
subs: (bhadra+āsana), s.m. Chair of state, throne; particular posture of a devotee while meditating (the legs being crossed and bent underneath the body and turned so as to bring the ankles into contact with the perinæum while the soles of the feet are held close to the sides

(bhadra+aśva), s.m. Name of a dwīpa; or of one of the four mahā-dwīpas into which the known world is divided; or of one of the nine khanḍas or smaller divisions into which the continent is distributed
(bhadra+aksha), s.m. A particular seed of which beads are made
s.f. Name of the third and fourth lunar asterisms: bhadra-parṇā, s.f. The shrub Pæderia fœtida
s.f. The tree Gmelina arborea; the shrub Pæderia fœtida
(bhadra-yava), s.m. The seed of Wrightia antidysenterica
s.m. A species of pine, Pinus deodora, and P. longifolia
s.f. Name of a Hindū goddess; an epithet of the goddess Durgā; the fragrant grass Cyperus pertenius or C. rotundus
s.f. The plant Cyperus rotundus; the creeping plant Asclepias pseudosarsa
s.m. The fragrant grass Cyperus pertenuis
(bhadr+odanī), s.f. The plant Sida cordifolia; the plant Uraria lagopodioides
s.f. Arabian jasmine, Jasminum sambac; the large Bengal creeper Gærtnera racemosa; the plant Vallaris dichotomus
v.n. To be clean or purified by shaving the head and beard after the death of a relative, or in a holy place

def: adj. & s.m. Good, estimable, worthy; fine, handsome, beautiful, pleasing, agreeable; fortunate, lucky; — a good or estimable man, &c.; goodness, good, worth, excellence, virtue; beauty, grace, elegance, neatness, pleasantness; gain, profit, advantage, benefit; nature, genius, reason; — the fragrant grass Cyperus pertenius; a species of pine, Pinus devadaru.
s.m. See s.v. bhadra.
s.m. One of the eighteen minor dwīpas or divisions of the world.
s.m. See s.v. bhadra.
s.m. Astrologer, palmister.
s.f. See s.v. bhadra.
s.m. Land prepared for the planting of sugar-cane; land ploughed during the ḵẖarīf and allowed to lie fallow till the cotton is sown; land ploughed from Asāṛh to Bhādoṅ for the rabīʻ sowings.

s.m. f. (?), Divine power, omnipotence supreme power; excellence, virtue, glory, fame, greatness, strength; splendour, beauty; prosperity, fortune; wish; effort, exertion; religious tranquillity or absence of passion; amorous pleasure, dalliance; pudendum muliebre, vulva.

s.m. Part, portion, member, share, lot, division; allotment, partition, distribution, apportionment; share in a partnership, or of an inheritance; share in kind; (in Arith.) division; tax, duty (as being the share of government); good portion, fortunate lot, good fortune, luck; fortune, lot, fate, destiny; — part of any whole, a fraction; a degree, or 360th part of the circumference of a circle
subs: s.f. Allotment of shares; apportionment in kind
adj. (f. -ī), Fortunate, lucky, propitious; prosperous; happy
bhāg khulnā (-kā), To be in luck, be fortunate or prosperous (syn. baḵẖt bedār honā)
(-kā) = bhāg denā and bhāg karnā; — bhāg lagnā, v.n. To be favoured by fortune, become fortunate; to make a show, give oneself airs
adj. According to, or in proportion to, the shares; held upon a joint tenure or share (land)

def: adj. & s.m. Running
subs: s.m. & adv. Running away, flight, scamper, stampede; — at a scamper, at full speed, helter-skelter

def: s.m. = bhagal, q.v.
s.m. The human skull
s.m. Affectation, hypocrisy; deception, deceit, trick, imposture, perfidy; acting, sham, simulation, disguise; — rotten or decayed corn
subs: bhagal gāṅṭhnā, bhagal nikālnā, v.n. To act a part, to affect; to pretend or sham (esp. poverty); to play a trick; to play the hypocrite.

def: adj. = bhagalī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Bedecked with skulls; — an epithet of Śiva.
s.m. Affectedness, hypocrisy, perfidiousness, deceit, &c. — bhagal.
s.m. Name of a district and town in Bengal.
adj. & s.m. Appertaining to Bhāgalpūr, of Bhāgalpūr; — a kind of silk and cotton cloth (originally) manufactured at Bhāgalpūr.
s.m. (in Pros.), A dactyl.
v.t. To cause to flee or escape, cause to run away, to put to flight, defeat, put to the rout
subs: v.t. To drive off, run away with, elope with.

def: s.m. lit. 'Rending the vulva'; a fistula in the pudendum muliebre, or in the anus; a boil in or close to the anus.
s.m. = bhagal, q.v.
s.f. Flight, escape from immediate danger, general emigration; panic, stampede ( = bhāgā-bhāg; bhājaṛ).
adj. & s.m. Righteous; pious; — devotee, pious or holy man, &c. (see bhakt); — mock representation, disguise, mask, religious play
subs: v.n. To act (a play), to imitate, mimic

v.n. To be initiated as a devotee, &c. ( = bhakt honā: the initiation, which is peculiar to the low tribes, consists in putting a necklace of beads round the neck, and marking a circle on the forehead; after which the initiated person is bound to refrain from the use of spirituous liquors, flesh, &c.).

def: s.m. A professional dancer; dancing boy (dressed up as a dancing girl); player.
intj. Your honour! your worship! your reverence!
adj. & s.m. = bhagwān, q.v.
s.m. Name of one of the most celebrated of the eighteen Purāṇas
subs: adj. After the manner of the Bhāgavat.

def: adj. & s.m. = bhagwān, q.v.
subs: s.f. Name of a philosophic poem (an episode of the Mahābhārat) held in high esteem by the Vaishnavas.

def: s.f. An estimable female; the goddess of fortune (Lakshmī); the goddess Durgā; — a sword; cannon.
s.m. = bhagel, q.v.
s.m. Overthrow, defeat, rout; runaway, &c. ( = bhaggū; bhagoṛā).
s.f. Flight; panic ( = bhāgaṛ)
subs: (-meṅ), To be stricken with panic; to fly helter-skelter.

ety: bhag, contrac. rt. of bhāgnā+ū = S. aff. uka
def: s.m. Runaway, fugitive, deserter; coward.
adj. & s.m. Sharing, participating; fortunate; — sharer, partaker, participator, partner, accomplice; heir; a fortunate person
s.m. Nephew ( = bhāṅjā, the form is peculiar to Bengal).
s.m. Sister's son ( = bhāṅjā, q.v.).
s.f. Partner; co-heiress; — sister ( = bhaginī; bahin).
s.f. Sister ( = bahin); — niece ( = bhāṅjī; — cf. bhaginā).
s.m. = bhagīrath, q.v.
s.m. Name of an ancient king (son of Dilip), who by his austere devotion caused the descent of the Ganges from heaven (hence he is called 'the father of the Gangeś').
s.f. Name of the Ganges; also of one of the three main streams or branches of the Ganges, viz. the great western branch.
adj. False, artificial, sham, mock, fictitious, invented, simulated, counterfeit, pseudo-; deceitful, perfidious
subs: s.m. Artificial or false jewels, trinkets.

def: s.m. Pretender, impostor, cheat; hypocrite; charlatan, quack.
adj. Favoured of the fates, fortunate; rich, prosperous; munificent, charitable.
s.f. Fortunateness, fortunate destiny, good fortune, prosperity.
s.f. Fortunate female.

ety: bhāg˚ = Prk. भग्गे(इ), or भग्ग(इ), fr. भग्ग = S. भग्न, p.p.p. of rt. भंज्
def: v.n. To run, to run off or away, fly, flee, take flight, escape, abscond, make oneself scarce
v.n. (prov.) = bhāgnā, q.v.

part. Broken, torn; defeated, vanquished; afflicted, wretched; despised, disregarded; — bhagnās, bhagnāś (bhagna+āśa), adj. & s.m. Of broken or disappointed hopes, disappointed, discouraged; — a disappointed person.
s.m. (?), The rich alluvial lands under the banks of the jamnā.
adj. See s.v. bhagna, bhagn.
s.m. = bhagwāṅ, q.v.
s.m. = bhagoṛā, q.v.
s.m. Runaway, &c. = bhaggū, q.v.
s.f. = bhāksī, q.v.
s.m. A tribe of ahīrs.
adj. & s.m. Running, flying; — one running, runaway, fugitive.
s.f. = bhaktāʼī, bhaktatā, q.v.
s.f. = bhagtānī, q.v.
s.f. Wife of a bhagat; — (ironically), lewd woman, prostitute, harlot.
s.m. Runaway, deserter, &c. ( = bhaggū, q.v.).
adj. = bhāgmān, q.v.
s.m. Kind of red ochre (syn. geru); cloth dyed with this colour (used by jogīs).
adj. & s.m. Fortunate, prosperous, happy, glorious, illustrious, adorable, divine, worshipful; — the Supreme Being, God; — any estimable or respectable person
subs: intj. God help or protect us (syn. naʻūẕu biʼllāh).

def: v.t. To cause to be driven away; to cause to drive away; to put to flight, &c. ( = bhagānā).
s.m. Fortune, luck, chance, fate, destiny ( = bhāg)
subs: adj. Not favoured by fortune, unfortunate, wretched, poor

def: s.f. Younger brother's wife, sister-in-law (cf. bahū).
v.n. To shiver, shake, tremble, totter, stagger (syn. thartharānā).

s.m. Brother; cousin; relative, kinsman; comrade, companion, class-fellow, member of the same caste, or fraternity; one connected by community of origin and joint interest in a common ancestral property; (in familiar phraseology) friend, my friend, dear, my dear (applied to persons of both sexes, and by children to their parents as well as parents to their children)
subs: s.m. The share or portion of a brother
s.m. Brotherhood, fraternity; a term applied to a village owned by descendants (brothers) from one common stock
(S. bhrātr̤i-vat), adv. Like a brother, befitting a brother, brotherly, fraternal
bhāʼī-bandh, bhāʼī-bandhu, s.m. Brethren, kindred, relations, friends; comrades, people of the same caste
s.m. A Hindū festival held on the twelfth day of Bhādoṅ badī, or dark fortnight in August

s.m. Relationship, connection, relation by blood or marriage; brotherhood, fraternity, community of brethren or of people from one stock; quality or property of brother, brotherly love, fraternal affection or kindness; fields or lands cultivated in common by a community of brethren; tenure in severalty; joint undivided estate
s.m. Share or portion of a brother = bhāʼī-aṅs.

def: s.m. = bhāʼī, q.v.; cf. also bhaiyā.
part. fem. of bhayā, q.v.
(Braj.), fem. of bhayā, and = huʼī, fem. of huʼā.
s.f. Younger brother's wife, sister-in-law (see bhaujī).
s.f. Younger brother's wife, sister-in-law (see bhaujī).
s.m. Living by alms, subsisting by charity; asking alms, begging, mendicity; that which is obtained by begging, begged food, alms, charity.

s.f. 'Daughter of Bhīm,' a patronymic of Damayanti; the eleventh day of the bright half of the month Māgh; a festival on that day (when ceremonies with til or sesamum are performed in honour of Bhīm).
adj. Fearful, terrible, &c. = bhayānak, q.v.
subs: v.n. To be terrifying; to be dreary or desolate.

def: s.f. Sister = bahin, q.v.
s.f. Sister, &c. = bahin (used by women).
s.m. = bahināpā, q.v.

ety: Prk. महिसो and महिसी; S. महिषः and महिषी
def: s.m. & f. Buffalo (female).
s.m. Buffalo (male)
subs: bhaiṅsiyā-dād (S. mahisha+dadru), s.m. A kind of ring-worm.

def: s.f. = bhaiṅs, q.v.
s.f. Dialect. form of bhaiṅs, q.v.
s.m. Tax or cess for the privilege of grazing buffaloes.
s.m. = bhāʼī-ćārā, q.v.s.v. bhāʼī.
s.m. = bhāʼī-ćārā, q.v.s.v. bhāʼī.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode.

adj. & s.m. Formidable, awful, horrible, terrible, terriffic, horrid, miserable; — terror, horror; the property of exciting terror, terribleness, formidableness, horribleness, dreadfulness; abhorrence, hatred; a form of Śiva (especially an inferior manifestation of that deity; — eight forms are enumerated, all of which allude to terrific properties of mind or body); — name of a river; — name of a rāg or musical mode calculated to excite emotions of terror (it is sung at early dawn in autumn, and is represented in pictures by Mahādeo with the Ganges flowing from the hair of his head and this rāg or musical deity issuing from his mouth).
s.f. The wife of Bhairav; a particular form of Durgā; name of a rāginī or musical mode sung in the morning.
s.f. = bhīrī; bahrī, qq.v.
s.f. = bhaṅbīrī, q.v.
s.m. = bhairoṅ; bhairav, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = bhairav, q.v.
s.m. Stunted crop.
s.m. Paying and receiving on the footing of one of a brotherhood or fraternity (cf. bhāʼī-baṭ).
s.m. Brother; comrade ( = bhāʼī, q.v. for compounds, &c.).

r: bhaiyā-pā, bhaʼīyāpā, bhayāpā, bhaiyāpat, bhaʼīyāpat, bhayāpat
def: s.m. Brotherhood; brotherly affection; friendship, &c. ( = bhāʼī-ćārā, q.v.).

r: bhaiyāpat, bhaʼīyāpat, bhayāpat, bhaiyā-pā, bhaʼīyāpā, bhayāpā
def: s.m. Brotherhood; brotherly affection; friendship, &c. ( = bhāʼī-ćārā, q.v.).
s.m. = bhāṅj, q.v.
adj. Dividing; — s.m. (in Arith.) Divisor.
s.m. (in Arith.), Division; — vessel, pot, dish, plate (syn. bāsan).
s.m. Sharing; possession; possessing or enjoying carnally.
s.m. Waiting upon, attendance, service; adoration, worship; hymn; — counting one's beads
subs: bhajan-gr̤iha, bhajan-ghar, s.m. Place of worship, temple
s.m. Reverence of disposition; attention to the needs of others, kind offices
s.m.f. Book for guidance in worship, liturgy
v.t. & n. To practise devotion, say prayers; to adore, worship; to sing or chant a hymn, to hymn; to recite, repeat.

def: v.t. = bhagānā, q.v.
s.m. Singer; adorer, worshipper.
